subjectLine: A quick July check for your home
previewText: A simple summer home check, one useful market thought, and a local Suwanee event.

paragraphs: I hope you had a safe and enjoyable Fourth of July weekend.
Summer has a way of moving quickly. Between vacations, family plans, yard work, and trying to stay cool, the weeks can get away from us.
So this week’s note is simple: one helpful thought about today’s real estate market, one quick home-maintenance check, and a local event you may want to enjoy with the family.
As always, if a real estate question comes up, I’m here to help you sort through it — one step at a time.

market.title: Preparation matters more than ever
paragraphs: When buyers have choices, they tend to slow down and compare homes more carefully.
That means the small details matter: clean rooms, completed repairs, good lighting, a comfortable temperature, and a price that makes sense beside nearby competition.
The simple version: sellers do not need a perfect home, but they do need a thoughtful plan. Good preparation can help a home feel like the easy choice.

homeownerTip.title: Give your air conditioner a five-minute check.
homeownerTip.intro: Georgia heat makes the air conditioner work hard. This week, take a quick look at a few basics:
items: Check whether the air filter needs replacing.
Make sure furniture or curtains are not blocking indoor vents.
Look around the outdoor unit and clear away leaves or plants.
Watch for unusual sounds, weak airflow, or water where it should not be.
homeownerTip.closing: Catching a small issue early is usually easier than discovering it on the hottest afternoon of the year. If something looks wrong, call a qualified HVAC professional.

localEvent.title: Suwanee Celebrates America 250
localEvent.intro: If you are looking for something local this weekend, here is one Gwinnett event worth checking out.
localEvent.copy: The City of Suwanee is hosting a patriotic community celebration on Saturday, July 11, from 3:00 PM to 9:00 PM at Town Center Park. The afternoon and evening include live music, family activities, friendly contests, food and artisan vendors, and a Liberty Lap for children with bikes, wagons, scooters, or strollers.
localEvent.tip: Tip: Bring lawn chairs, arrive early for activities, and check the official event page for weather or parking updates.

cartoon.imageAlt: A happy dog stretched across the coolest air-conditioning vent while the family looks amused
cartoon.caption: “The coldest seat in the house has been claimed.”
cartoon.closing: Every family has at least one cooling expert.
cartoon.imagePrompt: Create a horizontal 16:9, bright, warm, family-friendly cartoon illustration for a homeowner newsletter. Show a cheerful dog stretched comfortably across a floor air-conditioning vent in a clean suburban Georgia living room while a friendly family looks amused. Summer daylight, clean lines, natural colors, no logos, and no words or text in the image.
